contents.value:
```bash
KEY=value
DATABASE_URL=postgres://localhost/mydb
PORT=3000
```
**Environment variables** are key-value pairs. No spaces around `=`. Values are strings by default.

```bash
# This is a comment
# DATABASE_URL=old-value  # disabled
```
**Comments** start with `#`. Used for documentation and temporarily disabling variables.

```bash
SINGLE='literal $value'
DOUBLE="expanded ${VAR}"
UNQUOTED=no-spaces-allowed
```
**Quoting** — Single quotes are literal. Double quotes expand variables. Unquoted values end at whitespace.

```bash
BASE_URL=https://api.example.com
API_URL=${BASE_URL}/v1
FULL_URL=${API_URL}/users
```
**Variable interpolation** uses `${VAR}` inside double-quoted or unquoted values. Not all dotenv libraries support this.

```bash
PRIVATE_KEY="-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
MIIEpAIBAAKCAQEA...
-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----"
```
**Multiline values** use double quotes. Newlines inside quotes are preserved.

```bash
# PostgreSQL
DATABASE_URL=postgres://user:pass@host:5432/db
# MySQL
DATABASE_URL=mysql://user:pass@host:3306/db
# SQLite
DATABASE_URL=sqlite:///path/to/db.sqlite3
```
**DATABASE_URL** is the standard connection string format used by ORMs and database tools.

```bash
# .gitignore:
.env
.env.local
.env.*.local

# .env.example (commit this):
DATABASE_URL=
API_KEY=
```
**Security** — Never commit `.env` files with secrets. Use `.env.example` as a template.

```bash
# Load order (typical):
# 1. .env              (shared defaults)
# 2. .env.local        (local overrides)
# 3. .env.development  (environment-specific)
# 4. .env.development.local
```
**Loading priority** varies by framework. System env vars usually take highest priority.

```bash
PORT=3000
```
**PORT** specifies the network port for the application server. Common default: 3000 (Node), 8000 (Python), 8080 (Java).

```bash
NODE_ENV=development
NODE_ENV=production
NODE_ENV=test
```
**NODE_ENV** controls the Node.js runtime mode. `production` enables optimizations and disables dev tools.

```bash
API_KEY=sk_live_abc123def456
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_...
```
**API_KEY** stores third-party service credentials. Never commit to version control.

```bash
SECRET_KEY=your-256-bit-secret
JWT_SECRET=randomly-generated-string
```
**SECRET_KEY** is used for cryptographic signing (sessions, JWTs). Generate with a secure random method.

```bash
DEBUG=true
DEBUG=app:*,http
DEBUG=false
```
**DEBUG** enables debug mode. Some frameworks use boolean, others use namespace patterns (e.g., Express debug module).

```bash
LOG_LEVEL=debug
LOG_LEVEL=info
LOG_LEVEL=warn
LOG_LEVEL=error
```
**LOG_LEVEL** controls logging verbosity. Common levels: `debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error`, `fatal`.

```bash
HOST=0.0.0.0
HOST=localhost
HOST=127.0.0.1
```
**HOST** specifies the network interface to bind. `0.0.0.0` listens on all interfaces, `localhost` only local.

```bash
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379
REDIS_URL=redis://:password@host:6379/0
REDIS_URL=rediss://host:6380  # TLS
```
**REDIS_URL** connection string for Redis. `rediss://` for TLS connections.

```bash
SMTP_HOST=smtp.gmail.com
SMTP_PORT=587
SMTP_USER=user@gmail.com
SMTP_PASS=app-password
SMTP_FROM=noreply@example.com
```
**SMTP** variables configure email sending. Use app-specific passwords, not account passwords.

```bash
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=AKIA...
AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=...
AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=us-east-1
AWS_S3_BUCKET=my-bucket
```
**AWS** environment variables are recognized by the AWS SDK automatically. IAM roles are preferred for production.

```bash
CORS_ORIGIN=https://example.com
CORS_ORIGINS=https://example.com,https://app.example.com
CORS_CREDENTIALS=true
```
**CORS** settings control Cross-Origin Resource Sharing. Comma-separated for multiple origins.

```bash
ALLOWED_HOSTS=example.com,www.example.com
ALLOWED_HOSTS=*
```
**ALLOWED_HOSTS** (Django/similar) restricts which hostnames the server responds to. `*` allows all (insecure in production).

```bash
TZ=UTC
TZ=America/New_York
TZ=Europe/London
```
**TZ** sets the timezone. Use IANA timezone names. `UTC` is recommended for servers.

```bash
LANG=en_US.UTF-8
```
**LANG** sets the locale for language and character encoding.

```bash
BASE_URL=https://api.example.com
NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL=https://example.com
VITE_API_URL=https://api.example.com
```
**BASE_URL** is the root URL for the application. Prefix conventions: `NEXT_PUBLIC_` (Next.js), `VITE_` (Vite) for client-side exposure.

```bash
# Exposed to browser (Next.js):
NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=https://api.example.com
NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_ID=G-XXXXXXX

# Server-only (not exposed):
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://...
```
**NEXT_PUBLIC_** prefix exposes variables to the browser in Next.js. Without prefix, variables are server-only.

```bash
# Exposed to browser (Vite):
VITE_API_URL=https://api.example.com
VITE_APP_TITLE=My App

# Server-only:
DB_PASSWORD=secret
```
**VITE_** prefix exposes variables to the browser in Vite projects via `import.meta.env.VITE_*`.

```bash
# Create React App:
REACT_APP_API_URL=https://api.example.com
REACT_APP_VERSION=$npm_package_version
```
**REACT_APP_** prefix exposes variables in Create React App via `process.env.REACT_APP_*`.

```bash
VAR=          # empty string
VAR=""        # empty string (explicit)
# VAR         # not set (commented)
```
**Empty values** — An empty value (`=` with nothing after) is different from an unset variable.

```bash
QUOTE_VAR="value with \"quotes\""
NEWLINE_VAR="line1\nline2"
DOLLAR_VAR='price is $5'
```
**Escape sequences** — Use `\"` for quotes in double-quoted values. Single quotes prevent all interpretation.

```bash
export DATABASE_URL=postgres://localhost/mydb
export API_KEY=abc123
```
**export** prefix is optional and ignored by most dotenv libraries. Included for shell compatibility.

```bash
# Load .env and run command:
dotenv run -- npm start
npx dotenv -e .env.local -- node app.js
env $(cat .env | xargs) node app.js
```
**dotenv CLI** loads .env files before executing a command. Useful for tools that don't load .env natively.

```bash
# docker-compose.yml:
# env_file:
#   - .env
#   - .env.local

# docker run --env-file .env myimage
```
**Docker** can load .env files directly via `--env-file` flag or `env_file` in compose.

```bash
# System environment variables override .env:
# export PORT=8080  (takes precedence)
# .env: PORT=3000   (only used if not already set)
```
**Override behavior** — Most dotenv libraries don't replace existing env vars. System vars take precedence.

```bash
# Instead of .env files in production, use:
# - HashiCorp Vault
# - AWS Secrets Manager
# - Azure Key Vault
# - GCP Secret Manager
# - 1Password CLI
DATABASE_URL=op://vault/db/url  # 1Password reference
```
**Secret management** — In production, use dedicated secret managers instead of .env files.

```bash
# .env files should be UTF-8 encoded
# Most parsers expect Unix-style line endings (LF)
# Windows CRLF is usually handled but can cause issues
```
**Encoding** — Use UTF-8 with LF line endings for maximum compatibility.

```bash
# CI/CD variables (set in pipeline settings):
CI=true
CI_COMMIT_SHA=abc123
GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_...
NPM_TOKEN=npm_...
```
**CI/CD variables** are set by the pipeline environment, not .env files. Never store CI secrets in .env.

```bash
SENTRY_DSN=https://abc@sentry.io/123
```
**SENTRY_DSN** configures error tracking with Sentry. The DSN (Data Source Name) identifies your project.

```bash
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=xxx.apps.googleusercontent.com
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=GOCSPX-...
GOOGLE_CALLBACK_URL=http://localhost:3000/auth/callback
```
**OAuth credentials** for Google authentication. Keep client secret out of version control.

```bash
MONGO_URI=mongodb://localhost:27017/mydb
MONGO_URI=mongodb+srv://user:pass@cluster.mongodb.net/mydb
```
**MONGO_URI** connection string for MongoDB. `mongodb+srv://` for Atlas cloud clusters.

```bash
S3_BUCKET=my-bucket
S3_REGION=us-east-1
S3_ENDPOINT=https://s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
```
**S3 configuration** for file storage. Custom endpoint allows using S3-compatible services (MinIO, R2).

```bash
SESSION_SECRET=random-32-char-string-here
COOKIE_DOMAIN=.example.com
COOKIE_SECURE=true
```
**Session configuration** for server-side sessions. SECRET should be cryptographically random.
